indication: Heart failure with reduced ejection fraction, three-vessel coronary artery disease on medical management, annual surveillance for inducible ischemia
technique: Pharmacologic stress echocardiography was performed using dobutamine infusion protocol (5-10-20-30-40 mcg/kg/min). Resting and peak stress 2D and Doppler echocardiographic images were obtained in standard views.

protocol.symptoms: No chest pain. Mild dyspnea at peak dose.
protocol.ecg: Baseline: Atrial fibrillation with controlled ventricular response. Stress: Atrial fibrillation with rate to 112 bpm. 1 mm ST depression in leads V4-V6, non-diagnostic in the setting of baseline abnormalities.

restingImages.leftVentricle: Moderately dilated (LVIDd 6.1 cm). Global hypokinesis. Akinesis of the inferior and inferolateral walls. Hypokinesis of the lateral wall.
restingImages.valves: Moderate mitral regurgitation (3+). Aortic valve sclerosis without stenosis. Mild tricuspid regurgitation.
restingImages.rightVentricle: Normal size, mildly reduced function (TAPSE 1.6 cm).

stressImages.wallMotion: Inferior and inferolateral walls remain akinetic. Lateral wall hypokinesis unchanged. No new regional wall motion abnormalities. Anterior, anteroseptal, and septal walls demonstrate appropriate augmentation.
stressImages.contractileReserve: Mild contractile reserve in the non-infarcted segments.

doppler.mitralRegurgitation: Moderate at rest, stable with stress.
doppler.rvsp: 38 mmHg at rest, 50 mmHg at peak stress.

impression: Moderately dilated left ventricle with severely reduced systolic function (EF 30-35%).
Fixed wall motion abnormalities in the inferior and inferolateral walls consistent with prior infarction/scar in the RCA territory.
No new inducible wall motion abnormalities to suggest significant ischemia.
Mildly elevated RVSP with exaggerated increase during stress (38 to 50 mmHg).
Overall findings stable compared to prior stress echocardiogram (2019-09-16).
recommendations: Continue guideline-directed medical therapy for HFrEF and coronary artery disease
Repeat surveillance stress imaging in 1-2 years
comparison: Stress echocardiogram dated 2019-09-16 and TTE dated 2020-09-21. EF stable. Wall motion abnormalities unchanged. Mitral regurgitation progressed from mild-moderate to moderate.
